rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type contracts in component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use valid component type or implementation scope for annex placement
Ensure all AGREE variables are grounded in the current AADL model or explicit declarations
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when modifying AADL models
Validate scope consistency when inserting annex blocks
Preserve existing component connections and subcomponents
Ensure all identifiers used in AGREE expressions are valid in the current scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` for valid AGREE syntax
Place assumptions and guarantees in appropriate scopes based on component type or implementation
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Avoid inserting annex blocks in invalid scopes for the target component or implementation
